rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF REABLE MATHIS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joint Petition To Approve Settlement Agreement
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/02/2021 (no earlier date available). 1) Both petitioners must sign the petition. At least one of the petitioners must verify the petition. CRC 7.103. 2) The proof of service does not show notice was given to the court-appointed counsel for the conservatee. 3) The proof of service does not show notice was given to Daniel Conrad who requested special notice. Copies of the petition and supplements must also be served. 4) Paragraph 5 of the petition: Grayson-Holmon is not the conservator of the person. 5) Paragraph 6: What was the amount of the claim made against the surety? Please provide a copy of the demand letter or other correspondence describing the demand. 6) The proposed order requests CCP 664.6 relief, but neither the petition nor the proposed settlement agreement included this relief.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF STELLA DAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/2/21. If the statutory fee/commission is based on any amount other than the I&A, detailed schedules of receipts and gains and losses on sales must be included. [CRC 7.705(b) and 7.550(b)(6).] The proposed distribution in paragraph 31 of the second amended petition states that cash on hand is $394,034.34, but paragraph 23 shows that amount to be the total value of all assets, including the three items of tangible personal property still on hand and proposed to be distributed to the administrator. The total cash on hand for purposes of calculating the distribution should also include the $60 that the administrator mistakenly paid out of the estate to two charities he intended to support personally. The total proposed distribution amounts still do not total the net amount on hand for distribution. (Calculating the amounts based on each heir's fractional interest rather than a percentage might result in more accurate amounts.) The administrator still does not address what happened to the three items on the Partial I&A numbered 20-22 totaling $230. They were not shown in the first report as having been sold. The Probate Code Section 13100 affidavits have not all been filed or not filed conventionally in order for the Court to confirm that a certified copy of the death certificate is attached. [LRSF 14.36 G. and 14.60 E.] (The Partial (and only) I&A was filed on 4/25/16 (not 4/26/16).) The reserve will be limited to closing costs, potential tax liabilities, and expenses, not attorney fees. Endorsed filed copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the Examiner by NO LATER THAN 2/19/21.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROSE PAK
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/2/21. The estate is not ready to be finally distributed if the issue of waiving appraisal of an asset still needs to be determined. Furthermore, the accounting begins with an amount that differs from the two I&As filed on 11/6/17, the accounting period exceeds the number of years this Court permits [LRSF 14.35 A.], the petition caption does not clearly set forth all of the relief sought [CRC 7.102.], the statutory fee calculation appears to be incomplete and incorrect, no original bank statement(s) to verify assets on hand [14.35 D.], receipts for any prior distributions must be filed [LRSF 14.36 I.6.c.], assets appear to have been distributed that were not inventoried with any specificity, and if distribution is not pro-rata, consents must be filed [LRSF 14.36 I.6.a.ii.]. The accounting has not been fully reviewed, as it will need to be amended and filed as two separate accounting periods. A request for extraordinary compensation must be supported by a statement that sets forth all of statements required by CRC 7.702. No proposed order has been submitted. [LRSF 14.7 A.] The proof of service filed on 1/4/21 shows an "Order" as having been filed and to be heard on 2/2/21. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues (e.g., a fully amended first two-year account, proof of service, original statement along with statement re retention of the original statement, fee declaration, etc.) must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 2/18/21.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SALLY S.L. LAM
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Terminating Proceedings (No Assets Subject To Administration), Directing Return Of Funds (Prob. Code Sec. 850) And Discharging Administrator Cta
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible due to the recent stay at home order issued by the mayor. Please review the 8th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their full name. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) Bond was increased to $253,000.00. The petition alleges $253,398.38 assets on hand. At the prior hearings counsel was told to address bond. Counsel indicated bond has not been addressed and the family is having great difficulty. It is the duty of the fiduciary or the fiduciary's attorney, upon becoming aware that the bond is insufficient to apply immediately for an order increasing the bond. [LRSF 14.40]. 2) Petitioner filed this as a waiver of account but has not received a waiver from a trustee of the survivor's trust Puiseng Lam aka Besin Pui Seng Lam.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ERIK B. FAALAND
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/23/21. A copy of the Supplement without a filed stamp was delivered to the Examiner. A courtesy copy [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.] was to have been received by the Examiner no later than 1/22/21; it appears the Supplement was not filed until 1/22/21. Notwithstanding LRSF 14.C., in hopes of not having to continue the hearing again, the UNFILED copy of the Supplement was reviewed as a one-time courtesy. The Supplement actually amended the statutory compensation, which requires notice. [CRC 7.53(a).] Original statements were reportedly attached to the Supplement, not filed separately, although the unfiled copy of the Supplement had the executor's original signature so it is unclear if the originals are in the possession of the executor (or her attorney). A statement verified by the attorney or the fiduciary (whoever is in possession of the original(s)) regarding the retention of the original statement(s) must be filed and must include the language required by LRSF 2.11 M.4 (that the original(s) will be retained "for at least the period after the termination, including all appeals, of the case required for conventionally filed documents"). The itemized costs reflect three payments made prior to the filing of the initial petition, including what appears to be a duplicate payment of $55 for the initial petition and a will translation fee without any explanation (the will was in English). Furthermore, charges for the electronic filing service do not reflect WHAT was filed and some of those costs appear to be inconsistent or not reflective of standard E-filing costs. The Supplement does not set forth the net amount of cash distributable to each beneficiary (merely stating the fractional share is insufficient). [LRSF 14.36 I.6.] The revised proposed order does not appear to state the correct amount of cash after deducting fees, costs, and reserve. (The Corrected I&A had NOT been filed; it was merely attached to the Supplement.) (Providing courtesy copies of previously filed papers is helpful; it is not necessary to attach them to a pleading.) Any responsive pleading should stand on its own and not refer to issues raised by an Examiner or posted in tentative rulings. If these issues are addressed in an amendment, notice must be given to, or waived by, the other beneficiaries. Endorsed filed copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the Examiner by NO LATER THAN 2/11/21.
